title: Skill: corvee version-check + upgrade flow; inject usage verbiage into CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md
description:
Extends TTR-E09-S07 (installer skill) with two new responsibilities.

1. Version-check + upgrade. The skill should detect the locally-installed corvee, compare against the latest published release, and offer/perform an upgrade when out of date. Resolution order should mirror S07: 'go install ...@latest' for Go-installed copies; download the matching release asset for binary installs; 'git pull && make build && sudo install bin/corvee' for source builds. Skill must handle the path back to the original install location (go-bin vs /usr/local/bin vs ~/.local/bin) without clobbering anything else. Acceptable user prompt: a clear 'X.Y.Z installed, A.B.C available — upgrade? [y/N]'. CI-style automated upgrades behind a --yes flag for unattended use.

2. AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md bootstrap. Many projects keep an AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md at the repo root that AI agents read on session start. The skill should append a short, opinionated 'Using corvee in this repo' block to whichever of those files exists (creating one if neither does — but only on user confirmation). The block should:
   - Point at skills/corvee/SKILL.md for the agent loop
   - State the workspace path (.tasks/)
   - Note the canonical agent loop: sync → next --auto-claim → heartbeat → done
   - Note the JSON envelope + exit-code contract so the agent doesn't need to grep stderr
   - Mention the SQ-007 coverage gate and the no-third-party-libs rule from §9 so agents writing new code respect them

Open question — should this be part of 'corvee init' instead of a skill? Init already writes .tasks/{workspace,local}.json and .gitignore entries. Adding optional CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md bootstrap there means 'corvee init --with-agent-docs' or auto-detect-and-prompt. Pros: one command. Cons: corvee init writing outside .tasks/ is scope creep, opinionated, and tightly couples to a specific agent runtime. Lean toward keeping it in the skill (which can ask the user before touching anything) and leave init focused on the workspace contract. Capture the resolution in docs/spec-questions.md.

Note: this story assumes a stable public repo URL exists. Filed now so it's not forgotten; implement after the OSS move (TTR-E09-S08 ships) so download URLs and version-check endpoints point at the canonical home.
acceptance_criteria: Skill detects installed corvee version (via 'corvee version') and the latest published release
Upgrade preserves the original install method and location (go install / binary / source)
Skill supports --yes for unattended upgrade; default is interactive prompt
Skill detects an existing CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md and appends a 'Using corvee' block (idempotent — re-running doesn't duplicate)
skill offers to create one; never writes without confirmation
Open-question 'init vs skill' resolved in docs/spec-questions.md (new SQ entry)
End-to-end smoke: install an older release → run skill → version-check upgrades to current → CLAUDE.md gets the verbiage idempotently across two runs

journal.note: Version-check + upgrade flow preserving install method; idempotent CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md 'Using corvee' marker-fenced block. SQ-008 records the init-vs-skill resolution (skill-only).
